content:
Key Findings

  • Burnley Borough Council's total spending across five financial years, from 2021/22 to 2025/26, amounted to £355.2M.
  • The largest spending department, unnamed in the data, accounted for £137.5M, representing 38.7% of the total expenditure.
  • Planning & Transportation was the second highest spending area, with an allocation of £58.4M, or 16.4% of the council's total spend.
  • Environmental Services received £35.7M, making up 10.1% of the overall budget analysed.
  • Leisure & Culture services had the lowest specific departmental spend at £7.9M, representing 2.2% of the total.

Burnley's Financial Overview: £355.2M Across Five Years

Burnley Borough Council's financial activities from 2021/22 to 2025/26 reveal a total expenditure of £355.2M. This comprehensive analysis, based on published spending records, offers insight into how public funds are allocated across various services. The data covers a five-year period, providing a broad view of the council's financial commitments and priorities.

As a district council in Lancashire, Burnley Borough Council is responsible for a wide range of local services. These include planning, environmental health, housing, leisure, and democratic functions. Understanding the distribution of the £355.2M total spend helps residents and stakeholders see where their council tax contributions are directed and which areas receive the most significant investment.

Key Spending Departments: A Closer Look

An examination of Burnley Borough Council's departmental spending highlights specific areas of significant investment. The largest single spending department, which is not explicitly named in the provided data, accounts for a substantial £137.5M. This figure represents 38.7% of the council's total expenditure over the five-year period. This significant allocation underscores its critical role in the council's overall operations and service delivery.

Following this primary expenditure, Planning & Transportation services received the second largest share, totalling £58.4M. This represents 16.4% of the council's total spend. Environmental Services also commanded a notable portion of the budget, with £35.7M allocated, making up 10.1% of the total. These figures suggest a strong focus on infrastructure, development, and maintaining public spaces within the borough.

To provide a clearer picture of departmental spending, the table below details the top areas of expenditure by Burnley Borough Council:

Rank Department Amount % of Total
1 Unnamed Largest Department £137.5M 38.7%
2 Planning & Transportation £58.4M 16.4%
3 Environmental Services £35.7M 10.1%
4 Legal & Democratic £35.3M 9.9%
5 Regeneration & Housing £26.5M 7.5%
6 Other £23.8M 6.7%
7 Resources & Finance £20.1M 5.7%

Understanding Smaller Budget Allocations

While some departments receive substantial funding, other essential services operate with smaller, yet still significant, budgets. Resources & Finance, for instance, accounted for £20.1M, or 5.7% of the total spend. This department is crucial for the council's internal operations, ensuring financial stability and effective resource management. The Chief Executive & Corporate department, responsible for overall leadership and strategic direction, spent £10.0M, representing 2.8% of the total.

Leisure & Culture services, which encompass parks, sports facilities, and cultural programmes, received £7.9M, making up 2.2% of the total expenditure. This figure highlights the council's investment in community well-being and recreational opportunities, albeit a smaller proportion compared to other areas. The category labelled "Other" accounted for £23.8M, or 6.7% of the total. This category often includes cross-departmental projects, minor expenditures, or items not easily classified under specific departmental headings.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Burnley Borough Council about the specific services or projects funded by the largest unnamed spending department.
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to hear discussions on budget allocations and spending decisions.
  • Check the council's published contracts register to understand which suppliers are receiving significant payments for services.
  • Write to your local councillor about your concerns regarding the proportion of spending allocated to specific departments like Leisure & Culture versus Planning & Transportation.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data for Burnley Borough Council and other Lancashire councils yourself.
This article is based on Burnley Borough Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
